rationale: In the theory section, Berger and Milkman (2011) is cited as core prior work on why people share news content, which the authors explicitly contrast with their own focus on retelling dynamics. In the general discussion, the paper again invokes Berger and Milkman to interpret its findings, noting consistency with evidence that emotionally arousing content is more likely to be transmitted. This is more than a passing mention because the citation helps frame the research gap and situate the paper's interpretation of its results.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Berger and Milkman (2011) as prior word-of-mouth evidence that content characteristics, including emotional arousal, influence sharing. It builds from that foundation by shifting attention from what gets shared to how information is altered across successive retellings.
